response:
TEMPERATURE IS A MEASURE OF HEAT IN SYSTEMS.
SUNLIGHT SHINES ON SURFACES AND WARMS THE SKY.
STEAM RISES FROM BOILING WATER IN POT.
SNOW STOPS WHEN TEMPERATURE RISES ABOVE FREEZING.
SAND STAYS HOT UNDER THE SUN FOR DAYS.
SOIL SOAKS UP HEAT FROM THE SUNLIGHT.
SEAS SWELL AS THE WARM AIR RISES.
STARS SHINE WITH INTENSE THERMAL ENERGY.
SPRING SEASON BRINGS WARMER DAYS AND SUN.
STORMS STIR UP COLD AIR FROM ABOVE.
SUMMER SUN BEATS DOWN ON EVERY SURFACE.
ROLL THE DICE TO SEE IF IT STAYS HOT.
SILVER SHINES BRIGHT IN THE SUNLIGHT.
